channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bondi says she won't play politics as attorney general but doesn't rule out probes of Trump foes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pam Bondi, Donald Trump's pick for attorney general, sought to reassure Democratic senators Wednesday that her Justice Department would not prosecute anyone for political purposes but also refused to rule out the potential for investigations into adversaries of the Republican presi
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Coors Light is temporarily changing its name: Here's why
item.description: This year, Coors Light is turning the case of the Mondays into a literal case, with limited-edition packaging and designs.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, NewsNation

item.title: Fire tornadoes pose a threat in California. A fire lab shows how they work
item.description: As if they aren’t already facing enough, firefighters in California also could encounter fire tornadoes — a rare but dangerous phenomenon in which wildfires create their own weather.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump and Biden both claim credit for Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and President-elect Donald Trump are both claiming credit for Israel and Hamas agreeing to a ceasefire deal in Gaza on Wednesday after the White House brought Trump's Middle East envoy into negotiations that have dragged on for months.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, AAMER MADHANI and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: For LA water issues, misinformation spreads nearly as fast as the wildfires
item.description: A billionaire couple was accused of withholding water that could help stop Los Angeles' massive wildfires. Democratic leadership was blamed for fire hydrants running dry and for an empty reservoir. Firefighters were criticized for allegedly using “women's handbags" to fight the fires.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pennsylvania grandfather in legal battle over backyard coaster
item.description: Some people build swing sets for their grandkids but Michael Chorba of Columbia County, Pennsylvania, has taken that a step further. He's refurbished an old rollercoaster on his property.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Sack, KRON4

item.title: Mediators herald Gaza ceasefire and hostage deal. Israel says final details are in flux
item.description: DOHA, Qatar (AP) — Israel and Hamas have agreed to pause the devastating war in the Gaza Strip, mediators announced Wednesday, raising the possibility of winding down the deadliest and most destructive fighting between the bitter enemies.
item.dc:creator: NAJIB JOBAIN, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NY federal judge denies bail for two real estate brokers and their brother in sex trafficking case
item.description: A federal judge in New York City says two luxury real estate brokers and their brother accused of raping dozens of women over two decades will remain in jail to await their trial on sex trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The long struggle to establish Martin Luther King Jr. Day
item.description: The Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. gave his iconic “I Have a Dream” speech in 1963 on the steps of the Lincoln Memorial. He chose that location in part to honor President Abraham Lincoln as “a great American, in whose symbolic shadow we stand today.” Now, millions of people honor King in the same way.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Southwest pilot arrested for suspected DUI at Georgia airport
item.description: A Southwest flight was delayed for five hours Wednesday morning due to a pilot being arrested and charged with DUI, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg backs Carrie Underwood inauguration performance
item.description: The comments about Underwood’s inauguration performance are notable coming from Goldberg, who is commonly a harsh critic of Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 private lunar landers head toward the moon in a roundabout journey
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — In a two-for-one moonshot, SpaceX launched a pair of lunar landers Wednesday for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jumpstart business on Earth’s dusty sidekick.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Whistleblower says UFO retrieval program exists: NewsNation exclusive
item.description: In an exclusive interview with NewsNation, whistleblower Jake Barber believes he recovered alien technology.
item.dc:creator: Ross Coulthart, NewsNation

item.title: Puerto Rico asks Trump for help after Venezuela's Maduro threatens invasion
item.description: Puerto Rican voters have overwhelmingly chosen to keep their U.S. citizenship, granted in 1917 by an act of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Momnibus Bill in Oregon aims to help pregnant families, kids
item.description: "I've known this as a pediatrician and it's only been brought home as a legislator," Oregon State Senator Lisa Reynolds said, "we do better with upstream investments and preventative spending.
item.dc:creator: Elise Haas, NewsNation

item.title: Houston manhunt ends: Suspect accused of fatally shooting deputy is shot and killed by police
item.description: A manhunt ended in Texas when a Houston man accused of shooting and killing a deputy serving a warrant on him in the parking lot of a Dollar Tree, was fatally shot by police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coors Light is temporarily changing its name: Here's why
item.description: This year, Coors Light is turning the case of the Mondays into a literal case, with limited-edition packaging and designs.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Disabled Los Angeles fire victim escaped flames in wheelchair
item.description: Galen Buckwalter, who is paralyzed from the waist down, said he watched as the glow of the wildfire raced down the San Gabriel Mountains toward their home in the foothill community of Sierra Madre.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Disabled Los Angeles fire victim escaped flames in wheelchair
item.description: Galen Buckwalter, who is paralyzed from the waist down, said he watched as the glow of the wildfire raced down the San Gabriel Mountains toward their home in the foothill community of Sierra Madre.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Smugglers transported Mexican cartel drugs to Indianapolis: DOJ
item.description: Thwarted by the DEA, 11 drug traffickers have now been sentenced for their role in the major drug ring.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: Palisades Fire firefighters taking part in first-of-its-kind cancer study
item.description: The study comes after the International Agency for Research on Cancer officially classified firefighting as a carcinogenic profession.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Smith, KRON4

item.title: Officials tout Gaza ceasefire, hostage deal as Israel says details still in flux
item.description: Israel and Hamas agreed to pause the devastating war in the Gaza Strip, mediators announced Wednesday, raising the possibility of winding down the deadliest and most destructive fighting between the bitter enemies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: LA fires' heat, intensity driven by planet-warming pollution: Study
item.description: A major compounding factor was likely the downstream effects of two unusually wet winters the Southern California region saw in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, Fox 5

item.title: Firefighters offer behind-the-scenes look at heroic Eaton fire battle
item.description: As the firefight continues amid fears of strong winds rebuilding the crews are determined to keep moving forward.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, NewsNation

item.title: US population projections shrink from last year because of declining birth rates, less immigration
item.description: The Congressional Budget Office has shrunk its projections for the U.S. population in 30 years to 372 million residents, a 2.8% drop from last year, citing declining birth rates and less expected immigration.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: United Airlines flight returns to O'Hare after hitting coyote during takeoff
item.description: A United Airlines flight from Chicago to Phoenix had to return to O'Hare International Airport on Sunday morning after its landing gear struck a coyote during takeoff.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KRON4

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass posed for photos at a cocktail party as Palisades Fire exploded
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass is at the center of controversy after social media photos showed her posing at a cocktail party in Ghana as the Palisades Fire exploded.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire tornadoes a risk under California's extreme wildfire conditions
item.description: As if they aren’t already facing enough, firefighters in California also could encounter a rare but dangerous phenomenon in which wildfires create their own weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FDA proposes sweeping limits on nicotine in cigarettes
item.description: It's an 11th-hour plan from the Biden administration that will take years to go into effect, if it happens at all.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: FDA bans red dye No. 3 from foods: What to know
item.description: U.S. regulators on Wednesday banned the dye called Red 3 from the nation’s food supply, nearly 35 years after it was barred from cosmetics because of potential cancer risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New documentary reveals antisemitic hostage siege captured on camera at Texas synagogue
item.description: Unseen security footage from the 11-hour Colleyville synagogue hostage crisis, capturing terror and resilience, is shown for the first time in a new documentary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Relief, 21st-century style: As wildfires burn, GoFundMe becomes a repository of harrowing stories
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — They seem endless, these sapping stories of loss. A grandfather starts over in his 90s. A family loses their dream home. People who were already struggling are dealt new, brutal blows.
item.dc:creator: MATT SEDENSKY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michelle Obama signals she’s done with Trump
item.description: Michelle Obama is done with President-elect Donald Trump, if her decision to skip next week’s inauguration means anything.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, Fox 5

item.title: A 'Particularly Dangerous Situation' is forecast for fire-scarred Los Angeles area
item.description: Millions of Southern Californians were on edge as a final round of dangerous fire weather was forecast for the region on Wednesday, along with a rare warning of a “Particularly Dangerous Situation” for an area near where two massive blazes have killed at least 25 and destroyed thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dire fire warning for LA area pushed back as winds ease
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Easing winds delivered a brief but much-needed reprieve to firefighters Tuesday as they battled two massive blazes burning in the Los Angeles area, and the National Weather Service pushed back its unusually dire warning of critical fire weather until early the following day.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago Chefs Cook initiative aims to help culinary community impacted by Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: Chefs and restaurateurs across Chicagoland have stepped up to help by pledging a portion of proceeds from special menu items to the cause.
item.dc:creator: Lourdes Duarte, WGN 9

item.title: This popular hiking trail may hold clues to how Palisades Fire ignited
item.description: A beloved hiking spot north of Sunset Boulevard in Pacific Palisades is reportedly a point of interest for investigators looking into the origins of the costliest wildfire in the history of Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4
